title: lophostoma calophylloides
intro: Lophostoma calophylloides, also known as the "Milkflower Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and aiding in digestion. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to promote overall well-being and alleviate various ailments.
